title: HX711 Load Cell Precision Weighing Interface
design_question: How to build a precision digital scale using a strain-gauge load cell with a resolution of < 1 g over a 5 kg range?
selected_approach: TAL220B 5 kg load cell (1 mV/V sensitivity) wired to HX711 breakout (AVDD = 5 V, gain 128, channel A). HX711 DOUT on D3, CLK on D4. bogde HX711 Arduino library. Calibration: tare on power-up; apply 500 g known weight → compute scale_factor. Average 8 readings per display update. Mechanical: load cell mounted in an aluminium beam fixture; overload stop screws at 110 % capacity.
rationale: HX711 with gain 128 provides 24-bit resolution from a 5 V reference, achieving < 0.1 g equivalent noise on a 5 kg cell. The bogde library handles the clocked read protocol and averaging. TAL220B is a cost-effective, temperature-stable single-point load cell suitable for 3D-printed platform scales.

alternatives.reason_rejected: NAU7802 is more modern (I²C, lower noise) but HX711 is ubiquitous and has mature Arduino library support at lower cost.

alternatives.approach: MCU ADC (10-bit) with INA125 instrumentation amp
alternatives.reason_rejected: 10-bit MCU ADC gives only ~4.8 mV resolution — insufficient for < 1 g weighing; dedicated 24-bit ADC required.

verification_notes: FACT: 'HX711 24-Bit ADC for Load Cell and Wheatstone Bridge Amplification' confirms gain-128 provides < 0.1 g noise at 80 SPS and documents calibration procedure.
supporting_fact_titles: HX711 24-Bit ADC for Load Cell and Wheatstone Bridge Amplification
